headline_assessment: Jinhae Gunhangje is a prevention benchmark: very large visitor volume forces price control, booth transfer control, crowd capacity management, traffic response and emergency response to be designed together.
confidence: B+ for public prevention model and visitor scale; A/B for 2023 official outcome; B for 2026 preventive measures until post-event inspection results are collected.

short_answer.why_this_matters: Large-crowd spring festivals can succeed economically while still creating price-gouging, booth resale, crowd bottleneck and traffic risks. Jinhae is useful because public actors explicitly connect price discipline and safety management before the event.
short_answer.is_this_a_failure_case: No. It is a prevention and benchmark case. It should be used to compare whether strict booth rules and safety controls reduce complaints and incidents.
short_answer.what_to_collect_next: Inspection result counts, booth removal/sanction records, complaint logs, shuttle/bus-lane outcomes, crowd-control interventions and post-event satisfaction.

timeline.event: Changwon reported 4.5 million+ visitors, estimated 260 billion KRW economic inducement and no safety accidents after the festival resumed after four years.

Changwon announced price-stabilization measures including joint inspection TF, report center, menu weight/price display, card-payment controls, three-strike sanctions and booth-transfer prevention deposit.
Changwon announced a joint price-gouging TF for the 64th festival, checking 115 booths and nearby stores, and applying one-strike-out removal for violations.
MOIS inspected crowd, traffic, emergency response, fire/electrical/gas facilities, bottlenecks, maximum capacity control, detours, free shuttles and bus lanes.

prevention_model.control: price/weight display, price-gouging TF, report center, one-strike or strike-based sanctions
booth transfer/resale prevention, deposit or sanction, permitted booth scope
maximum capacity management for dense locations such as Yeojwacheon and Jungwon Rotary, immediate entry control and detours
free shuttle buses, bus-only lanes, weekend congestion planning and route guidance
multi-agency inspection of emergency rescue, fire, electrical and gas risks

why_chain.label: Scale
why_chain.finding: Millions of visitors create both economic opportunity and operational risk.

why_chain.label: Price risk
why_chain.finding: Large demand can reward opportunistic pricing unless price/weight disclosure and sanctions are visible.

why_chain.label: Booth resale risk
why_chain.finding: Booth transfer/resale can create hidden cost pressure and weaken official price control.

why_chain.label: Crowd/traffic risk
why_chain.finding: Dense photo spots and synchronized visitor movement require capacity limits, detours and transport management.

why_chain.label: Benchmark use
why_chain.finding: Post-event data should show whether strict prevention reduces complaints, incidents and dissatisfaction.

documents_to_collect_next: Number of one-strike removals, warnings, corrections or business suspensions
Safety-control log for Yeojwacheon, Jungwon Rotary and other dense spots
Shuttle/bus-lane ridership and traffic delay data
